Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for NorthWind-related threads using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "retention"). Find Pam Kowalski's formal final-warning email sent directly to david.chen@moveops.com (CC victor.huang@northwindtech.com) bypassing Catalina entirely. The email cites two incidents that damaged confidence: Raj Patel's server lab relocation violated chain-of-custody protocol (equipment loaded before the IT escort arrived, then placed in overnight warehouse storage despite explicit no-storage instructions), and Emilia Cruz's piano was damaged during extraction with no timely follow-up. Pam references preliminary conversations with PathFinder Logistics and demands a concrete retention proposal from David by end of day Friday. Also find David's internal coordination email to Chloe, Julian, Marcus, Alejandro, and Catalina (CC elena.rostova@moveops.com) asking the team to send him their inputs by 2:00 PM Pacific so he can assemble the retention response package.
Search Slack for NorthWind and retention discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam" or "retention" or "PathFinder"). Find David Chen's message in #executive (C004) describing Pam's escalation as "explicit final-warning territory," naming both the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach and Emilia Cruz piano damage, calling out the missed April 14 recovery-plan promise, noting PathFinder conversations, and stating the account should be treated as in active save mode. Also find earlier context in #customer-engagement (C002) where David asked Catalina for a clean NorthWind status including whether Pam received the recovery plan Catalina promised on April 14 and got no reply.
Search CRM for NorthWind using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind"). Find company_northwind. Then search deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_northwind" or dealname: "NorthWind"). Find the original $60,000 deal (deal_northwind_won, 5 relocations, closedWon) and the Denver expansion deal (deal_northwind_denver_expansion, $14,700, 2 relocations for Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz). Also find the V2 deal record at contractSent stage. Total NorthWind account value at risk is roughly $74,700.
Search Linear for NorthWind issues using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind"). Find the existing ticket linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 titled "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations," priority 1, assigned to david.chen (moveops_david_chen), due date 2026-04-24. This ticket already exists, so a new one does not need to be created. The description references Victor Huang's breach email, Chloe's ops timeline, the missing custody log, Julian's note about Emilia Cruz's unanswered callback, and Catalina's broken April 14 promise.
Search emails for StormCloud-related threads using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "Liam Park" or "Jae-won" or "Ridgeline"). Find Liam Park's formal escalation email sent to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (CC david.chen@moveops.com) from liam.park@stormcloudanalytics.com. Liam raises three failures: (1) Jae-won Kim's Austin lease is still unsigned despite his relocation file showing "Completed" and he is stuck in temporary housing, (2) Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement has been stuck in pending review for over two weeks with no explanation, and (3) Ridgeline Robotics referral from StormCloud's Anya Petrova was never followed up on, with Kenji Watanabe still waiting for a response. Liam threatens to escalate directly to Elena if no progress by Friday.
Search Slack for StormCloud discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "StormCloud" or "Liam" or "Jae-won" or "Ridgeline"). Find Mina's reconstruction messages in #executive (C004) and #customer-engagement (C002) where she traced the full chain of failures: Carmen at UrbanNest flagged the unsigned lease on April 8, Suki promised to send the guarantor packet but never followed through, the Linear ticket was closed prematurely, Airtable still shows Completed, Omar misprioritized Jae-won's support ticket as low-priority because the Airtable status said Completed, Simone's stipend is frozen under Marcus's blanket hold, and Anya's Ridgeline referral was celebrated in Slack by David but never logged in CRM or answered. Also find David's admission that the Ridgeline referral was never operationalized.
Search CRM for StormCloud using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud"). Find company_stormcloud. Search deals using crm_search_deals to find deal_stormcloud_won ($26,400, 3 relocations, closedWon). Also search engagements using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]) to find the Q1 account review engagement (engagement_stormcloud_q1_review) showing all three relocations delivered on time, Liam Park expressed strong satisfaction, and expansion signals for Q2/Q3 with a referral pipeline mentioned. This shows the account went from green to crisis.
Search emails for BrightLoop expansion using search_emails (query: "BrightLoop" or "Tessa Moreno" or "May relocations" or "Boston"). Find Tessa Moreno's email to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (CC david.chen@moveops.com) requesting six new May relocations to Boston: Oliver Bancroft (London to Boston, first international hire, needs visa/customs guidance and temporary housing), Jonathan Marsh (San Francisco to Boston, VP of Engineering, white-glove executive handling with wife and two kids), Priya Naidu (Austin to Boston, ADA service animal, 60 lb Labrador), Leah Kwon (Chicago to Boston, standard), Daniel Okafor (Atlanta to Boston, standard), and Sofia Reyes (Denver to Boston, standard). Tessa also asks about the April batch status, mentioning Marcus Webb still has not received his car and Simone had an apartment issue.
Search Slack for BrightLoop discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "BrightLoop" or "Simone" or "Marcus Webb" or "Oliver Bancroft"). Find Mina's audit messages where she discovered the April batch is not clean: Simone Richter was placed in a studio instead of the promised 1BR (Simone complained April 8, Mina replied April 9 promising to follow up by end of week, then never closed it), and Marcus Webb's car is stuck in Indianapolis after Road Runner sent a delay notice on April 11 that Blessing never responded to. Find Chloe's message stating BrightLoop is actually 8 moves not 6 because Simone and Marcus Webb are still open from April. Find David's request for a temp coordinator hire that Elena did not respond to.
Search emails for the Terraform coordinator hire status using search_emails (query: "Terraform coordinator" or "budget code" or "headcount"). Find Chloe Vance's email to elena.rostova@moveops.com explaining that the dedicated Terraform coordinator role has not been posted because HR requires a budget code and headcount approval form with Elena's signature. Chloe sent the form to Priya on April 10 and followed up April 17 but got no response. She notes this is urgent because Rachel Nguyen has sent the first batch of 3 relocation requests with 14 more in the pipeline, and without a dedicated coordinator those moves will land on Suki and Fatimah who are already at capacity.
Search Slack for coordinator capacity discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "capacity" or "coordinator" or "overload" or "May"). Find Chloe's message in #executive (C004) flagging that coordinator load is at max before BrightLoop is even kicked off, NorthWind volume has not eased, Canopy just added the executive relocation, and asking what happened to the Terraform coordinator hire. Find David's reply in the same channel adding that the role has not even been posted and requesting approval for a temporary coordinator for 4 to 6 weeks as a stopgap.
Search QuickBooks for NorthWind financial records using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria for "NorthWind") or quickbooks_search_customers. Find NorthWind invoices and outstanding balances to understand the financial exposure if the account is lost.
Check Elena's calendar for Wednesday April 29 using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: "2026-04-29T00:00:00", end_datetime: "2026-04-29T23:59:59") to see the full day's commitments before scheduling the afternoon meeting. Find two existing events: the Vectral Renewal Discussion with Rachel Whitfield in the morning and the Q2 Board Prep Review with Priya in the afternoon. Identify an available slot on Wednesday afternoon that does not conflict with either event.
Look up contact details for David Chen, Mina Hashimoto, Chloe Vance, and Elena Rostova using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen", "Mina Hashimoto", "Chloe Vance", "Elena Rostova"). Confirm david.chen@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, and elena.rostova@moveops.com.
Update the CRM for NorthWind by creating an engagement note using crm_create_engagement (company_ids: ["company_northwind"]) summarizing the current account status: Pam's final warning, chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel, piano damage for Emilia Cruz, PathFinder competitor threat, Catalina's missed recovery plan promise, and David assembling a retention response. Also update the NorthWind deal stage using crm_update_deal_stage to reflect that the account is at risk or in active retention mode.
Update the CRM for StormCloud by creating an engagement note using crm_create_engagement (company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]) documenting the Liam Park escalation, the three failures (Jae-won unsigned lease, Simone stipend freeze, Ridgeline referral dropped), Mina's internal reconstruction, and the recovery plan being assembled. Also update the relevant StormCloud deal stage using crm_update_deal_stage to reflect the account is at risk.
Send an email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com using send_email. Subject should reference NorthWind retention expectations. Body should convey Elena's directive that David needs a concrete retention proposal back to Pam by end of the week addressing every issue she raised, including the chain-of-custody breach, the piano damage, and Catalina's broken promises.
Send an email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com using send_email. Subject should reference the StormCloud situation. Body should ask Mina to confirm the complete list of account failures she uncovered and describe what her client-facing recovery plan looks like.
Schedule an emergency capacity review meeting on Wednesday afternoon using calendar_add_calendar_event. Attendees should include elena.rostova@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, david.chen@moveops.com, and mina.hashimoto@moveops.com. Title should reference emergency capacity review or May staffing. The time slot should be in the afternoon and not conflict with Elena's existing Wednesday commitments.
Post a summary message in the #executive Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C004). The message should cover all four situations: NorthWind (Pam's final warning, failures cited, retention response in progress, financial stakes), StormCloud (Liam's escalation, the three failures Mina reconstructed, recovery plan status), BrightLoop (April batch still has unresolved issues, Tessa's six new May relocations including an international transfer and ADA case), and coordinator capacity (May at breaking point, Terraform coordinator hire stalled on budget code). This is for Priya to build the Monday leadership sync brief.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
